staticCommands.name: analyze
staticCommands.description: Check project(s) for workflow analyzer violations.

values.name: Workspace Path
values.description: Path to the folder where to search for a solution file or RPA projects.

options.description: Override severity for all analyzer rules. Allowed: Off, Error, Warning, Info, Verbose. Default: Error.

(Do not use this option for production purposes) Absolute path to a JSON file where the command will be captured instead of being executed.
The resulted JSON file can be used to execute the captured command with `uipcli run`.
Useful to understand what the `arguments JSON file` should contain, when executing `uipcli run`.
Disable built in nuget feeds.
Disable telemetry data.

Pass governance policies containing the Workflow Analyzer rules. Policies can be downloaded from Automation Ops, or extracted from the exported compressed zip file from Studio. For details, please consult the documentation.
Url of your identity server. This is only required for PaaS deployments.
A comma-separated list of rules to be ignored by the analysis procedure.
Language for the translated logs.
Path to the NuGet.Config file containing feeds.
(Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with id, secret and scope(s) for external application.
(Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with the refresh token and client id.

(Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The external application id. Must be used together with organization name, secret and scope(s) for external application.

(Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The space-separated list of application scopes. Must be used together with organization name, id and secret for external application.

(Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The external application secret. Must be used together with organization name, id and scope(s) for external application.
(Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator refresh token used for authentication. Must be used together with the account name and client id.
(Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator folder name.
(Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator password used for authentication. Must be used together with the username.
(Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator tenant.
(Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator URL.
(Optional, useful only when you have dependencies from a library in Orchestrator) The Orchestrator username used for authentication. Must be used together with the password.
The full path to a JSON file where the result json file will be created. Otherwise print it to the standard console.
Fail when any rule is violated.
Specifies what types of log messages to output  One of the following values: None, Critical, Error, Warning, Information, Verbose. (default Information)
Treat warnings as errors.

staticCommands.name: delete
staticCommands.description: Delete assets from an Orchestrator instance (based on asset name).

values.name: Assets File
values.description:
The file containing the assets to be deleted.

The following is a sample csv file. The column names are required! Only the first column is used but you need to at least have empty columns in place.
name,type,value
asset_1_name,,asset_value # we can have comments
asset_2_name,integer,
asset_3_name,,
asset_4_name,credential,username:password

staticCommands.name: deploy
staticCommands.description: Deploy packages to an Orchestrator instance, optionally publishing them to a set of environments.

values.name: Package(s) Path
values.description: The path to a folder containing packages, or to a package file.

values.name: Orchestrator URL
values.description: The URL of the Orchestrator instance.

values.name: Orchestrator Tenant
values.description: The tenant of the Orchestrator instance.

options.description: The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with the refresh token and client id.
The external application id. Must be used together with organization name, secret and scope(s) for external application.
The external application secret. Must be used together with organization name, id and scope(s) for external application.
The Orchestrator password used for authentication. Must be used together with the username.
The Orchestrator refresh token used for authentication. Must be used together with the account name and client id.
The Orchestrator username used for authentication. Must be used together with the password.
The Orchestrator organization name. Must be used together with id, secret and scope(s) for external application.
The space-separated list of application scopes. Must be used together with organization name, id and secret for external application.

(Do not use this option for production purposes) Absolute path to a JSON file where the command will be captured instead of being executed.
The resulted JSON file can be used to execute the captured command with `uipcli run`.
Useful to understand what the `arguments JSON file` should contain, when executing `uipcli run`.
When set to true, it will create a process in the respective folder as per the given entry points. (default true)
Disable telemetry data.

Define the specific entry points to create or update a process. This is the filePath of the entry point starting from the root of the project. For classic folders only one entry point can be specified, for each environment it will be created or updated a process with the specified entry point.

(Only for classic folders) The comma-separated list of environments to deploy the package to. If the environment does not belong to the default folder (organization unit) it must be prefixed with the folder name, e.g. AccountingTeam\TestEnvironment
Url of your identity server. This is only required for PaaS deployments.

Ignore the conflict when deploying a library package with the same version as an existing one. By default, the deployment will fail if a library with the same version already exists in the Orchestrator instance.
Language for the translated logs.
The Orchestrator folder name.
Set the process description to the specified value. Takes highest priority over any other description source.
Optionally provide a custom name for the process to be updated/created

Optionally provide a .csv file with custom names for the processes to be created/updated.

The following is a sample csv file. The column names are required!
PackageName,EntryPointName,ProcessName
MyPackage,Main.xaml,MyProcess
YetAnotherPackage,CustomEntryPoint.xaml,YetAnotherProcess
Specifies what types of log messages to output  One of the following values: None, Critical, Error, Warning, Information, Verbose. (default is Information)
Use the package description as the process description. On create this is the default behavior. On update this overrides the existing process description with the package description.
